reviews.review_description: I had played this a year ago but had to refund this game do to the trial system in quest store not allowing the game to start after the purchase of the game. I do not know who removed the trial download from list but it allowed me to repurchase this because it will launch now finally.

This game is a masterpiece in standalone vr games.if you wanted to play nms vr but can't,this is for you.you can bounty hunt,deliver packages or even better a criminal to steal spaceships.the one dev making this game works so hard and has a massive roadmap for major updates.although it isn't multiplayer it is still amazing for people who love action or love chilling on alien planets.

Note for dev:if you are reading this if possible you should add land vehicles (hoverbike,card etc)and more weapons of lots of kinds

This game is a vr fantasy come to life. Customizable ships better ships to buy if you wanna keep one after stealing one you like. Different homes to buy and chill in(your starter apartment has an amazing view and you can take a space bath) a lot of the mechanics are super cool like buying things from replicators the way everything is formed in like a futuristic resin printer. Soundtrack fits the theme well. As others have said this is like an immersive retro space game you can walk into it’s really really cool and you can create your own fun

An utterly stellar magnum opus by a lone developer with the type of creative vision that big name studios abandoned a long time ago. This is one of the few games/sims that actually fulfills VR's promise: to give you a second life in a strange new universe...

reviews.review_title: The most fun,funny and chaotic game ever
reviews.review_description: Ive got multiple reasons why its fun well its no man's sky in vr except none of the boring bits. If (im saying if coz idk if it is or isn't in the game) this game let's you build your own ships that would be so fun and cool. I have no idea how this isn't popular i don't have a definite answer but if this game was on yt or tt I would've played it years ago. Tldr its fun. Buy it now!!!

this is the closest game you're gonna get to star citizen and although the graphics aren't the best and there's no space stations the developer has already planned on doing making space stations in upping the graphics and new ships new missions and guns as well and hopefully the developer will add multiplayer and maybe after a few years the developer can add new star systems. the game is very fun but the game will get very repetitive the developer can try and use the money and get a developer team because right now it is a single developer but when the developer gets a team updates will be pushed out a lot faster. props to the developer.

reviews.review_title: A Rare Space Adventure for Standalone
reviews.review_description:
One of my favorite VR experiences was Outer Wilds -- it was the reason I got my VR legs, which you're also gonna need for this one.

This is the only game on standalone Quest that has come close to giving me that "Outer Wilds" feeling... exploring a vast yet miniature solar system -- that feeling of scale -- landing on unique planets, and just taking in the view. Day/night cycle! Real time shadows!  All on an Android phone strapped to your face! Wow!

Although it is a work in progress (the UI's graphic design doesn't seem like a top priority, and there's clearly lots of placeholder content) -- this is the kind of game I can still get a lot out of. I just like putting on a podcast and delivering packages in my spaceship. I'm saving up for a bungalow on Eno, then I don't know what.

Screenshots don't do it justice, really. It has a simple art style, but it's got character. I like the buildings and the vehicles, and the weird humanoids walking around. 

Looking forward to whatever else the dev wants to add to it, but it's worth playing now. It's rare when a standalone Quest game contains a world you think of returning to when you're outside of VR, but I keep coming back.

A sandbox, open map, flying, space simulation. This game is what vr was made for. What sets this game apart is the open world that has no loading screens. There are multiple planets and moons you can explore and complete missions on. It's actually insane to me that this game is not more popular. You would think games like this would be priority number one for vr gaming. Either way, I highly recommend this game. If you need something to scratch that space, flying or open world itch. This is it. It's worth every penny.

Im gonna be completely honest, this might just be one of the best vr games I've ever played and I'm not kidding. You get:
-Dynamic Job payment depending on how long it took
-NPC events, such as police chases, shootouts, etc
-Bounty hunting jobs
-6 planets with 5 moons, each one unique
-Asset customization, player, ships, guns, suits, etc
-Good real-time Shadows on quest
-And a dev who truly cares about his players and the game without adding microtransactions who also is fully transparent about what he plans to add and change next
All for $15. Though, I must say that after you buy the most expensive house (250,000 in-game credits) there isnt really a grind anymore, though we can depend and trust the dev to add more grind-worthy content to the game in the coming updates

this game is truly a hidden masterpiece it's so addicting and so fun i would love to see more creatures and more planets in the future and more things to do on the planets but right now it is a very addicting game that i love to just hop in and relax

This game is really something special.  Even just in its infant stages, you have a bustling universe where you can hop in your space ship and travel between planets with no loading screens in between.  There is a ton of fun to be had and a ton to do, even at this stage of the game! Start out doing cargo missions in your basic, but reliable ship.  You got bounty missions, go hunt down criminal ships in space or in the planet. Or sometimes you will be sent to hunt down robots where you get out on foot to hunt them down. Or just toast them from the sky with your ship! Don't forget to go down and pickup the loot they drop, you can get some good weapons and rechargeable battery packs!

The open-ended style with a mission board that keeps feeding you missions is awesome. The road map has a ton of amazing features. I think maybe the biggest hang for some people might be the more basic graphic style. I will happily take a more basic graphic approach for the sheer top notch quality in gameplay and high performance.

I hope to see this game blow up and get the funding it deserves and see it through to completion. A lot of the core mechanics of a bustling universe are there, and now it's really all about some QoL improvements and adding the variety that will make the game flourish!

It's astounding that this is made by just one dev. Great job to the dev and I'm wishing continued success with this one!

This is already a masterpiece even though it is clearly in early development. I love flying the ship around and exploring unique and cool planets, delivering packages and hunting down bounties. I can't wait for the next updates. This game also desperately needs multiplayer with a player driven economy.

this game, is one of the better early access VR games I ever played. from the fact it doesnt hold your hands to the easy and simple controls, along with the cool planets. this game is impressive.. now the caviots.. you don't actually die, instead, the sprites derender, like the trees and robots, but you can still move and fly ships. also the graphics, while I understand its early solo developed, a more realistic atmosphere for the planets will be appreaciated... also I have a request you add a "creative" mode or a God mode. other than that.. simply.. amazing.

This game is really good and can be even better. One suggestion is some bounties you can retrieve and put the npc/s into your ship with space cuffs after stunning them or something. But overall fun and immersive and can always do something interesting.

. I don’t write many reviews.  In my 50’s and PC and VR games is one of my hobbies.  And I work full time.  I recall  the days of Atari 2600.  Escaping into this world is fantastic. I love open world games, especially space games and play elite, x4, NMS the like.   It is extraordinary that one person has developed this.  Being able to just get up in your own apartment or home, get In your ship and go where you want and explore. I love the cockpit experience with the buttons and custom lighting- the cool view out the windows.  So many more little touches.I dint need I overly complicated and complex.  Just a reason why I’m doing what I’m doing.   The potential for this games is crazy!  And the love and passion of this developer is apparent.  As it is now it is a fun game .Based on what I see I trust the developer will continue to improve his “baby”.    This is a no brainer to add to your library. These are the people that need support.  I look forward to more improvements.  Thank you for your work.   I can relax a bit after work in this game  because of the countless hours you put in.  You are appreciated.

I mean the concept is amazing. A few helpful hints. You're gonna want to hit the flyaway store and get yourself an environmental protection suit. It's gonna cost almost half your starting credits. Start off with lower paying delivery jobs. The higher the pay the further the distance you'll have to travel. You can purchase batteries but I'm not entirely sure if they're for the ship, assuming they are. But it's just easier but the suit so you can survive spaces without domes, and build up with the smaller carrier jobs. Also place packages at the yellow points not the green one.

reviews.review_title: Hidden gem I was looking for!
reviews.review_description: Amazing game! Today I flew past a asteroid field and decided to take a closer look, I flew close to one of the bigger asteroids and was able to land, amazing! I got out and looked out into the void of space before getting back into my ship and delivering the packages to the planet close by. This game takes me back to days of Elite Frontier that I played on my Amiga. Thank you developer :)

There is the feeling of being in another world and this is what VR should always do: there are many limitations and the game is still in development but DEV continues on this path and perseveres because what you are doing, with so much effort, is something magical.
5 stars because it's a hardworking dev who truly cares about the project.
enjoyed messing around so far, while it's not a completely fleshed out game yet, I really enjoyed all of it so far, and it has tons of potentials for the future

I am salavating over the potential of this one, even with the basic/styalized graphics pallette.  It's clear they're more focused on the gameplay and the worldbuilding, which is appropriate.  I can't wait to see more!

Bigger ships with interiors might be fun... just sayen
Exactly what i was looking for !!!

reviews.review_title: The game I always wanted to do myself
reviews.review_description:
This is my first time writing a review. 
I really love this game and the idea behind it. Those who know the complexity of the mechanics involved in this game will understand why I am impressed of how currently it works. Being a solo developer work makes it more incredible. 
Developer shows a real passion and great commitment in improving the game, taking his time to make sure everything works before deploying an update. 

The thing I love the most is the scale of this universe. It is small so you can do things in shirt game sessions (I can only afford small play time sessions at night, after work and once the kids are sleep), but at the same time it feels big and really “alive” with NPCS around in the right density. 
The flight mechanics is well balanced and adapted to the touch controllers. 
I’m not the first to say this, but it feels like the idea of “star citizen” for oculus. 
C’mon! Let’s support the developer downloading this app, the more players the better it will become!
If you check the game webpage you’ll be impressed of the upcoming changes (spherical planets and seamless transitions between planets is what I most look forward to.
Definitely an app for any space sim lover!

reviews.review_title: This... will just never be good.
reviews.review_description: I see what you doing. It's a interesting concept  put together really rudimentary at the moment. Bare bones on everything. Alot needs to be done to flesh this out enough to get a player base. I feel bad for all the work that has been done and players money that has been spent. After reading about the developers money problems and getting no game updates in forever I don't see this being any more then what it is now. Just a regret sitting in my collection. I say this with my honest opinion so take it for what it is. Not recommended. DONT GET UPSET BRUV. JUST PROVE ME WRONG.

developer_response.body:
Why would you do this to me? To my game? I've been working for months on the next update. It is a TON of work. It's a complete rebuild of the entire game world. Open solar system. Spherical planets. No more jump gates. A new save system. New home/property system. New cargo system. Wildlife. Moons.  And the MAIN reason I'm holding off on releasing it is because I want all of these new things to work together correctly and to ensure the new save system is reliable.  I work every single day that I can on Revria. I have spent thousands of hours. I have written over 100k lines of code. FOR YOU. For the people that bought my game. I am earning almost NOTHING from this game, yet I work tirelessly because I still believe in this dream.  I am extremely insulted and really wish you would have made even the tiniest effort to contact me for an update about doing this. There have been updates on the Discord, subreddit, and the website to keep everyone informed of what's happening.
---
Watch me, my homey.

reviews.review_title: Fun game right now, crazy potential!
reviews.review_description: Revria is already fun, and has the potential to be one of the best on the quest store. The game has a solid foundation, and a dedicated dev with a vision.

reviews.review_title: A world I can live in
reviews.review_description:
Even with it's very unfinished look and feel, this game has brought me a higher level of immersion than 90% of the VR games I've played out there. I feel it is mainly because this game was developed with excellent fundamentals from the interactions, to the worlds that truly feel alive and busy with life (even with 0 voicelines!) 

I've only spent a couple hours playing so far, but every time I took my headset off it felt like waking up from a different life. Though not exactly comparable, the last time a VR game made me feel like this was Skyrim VR.

Support the dev so he can improve the game!

UPDATE:

Revria reminds me a little of the “Mercenary” series, a game from the 1990s for the Commodore 64 and Amiga.

To me, some obvious improvements would be (I’m sure many are on the dev’s list already):

- Multiplayer
- Graphics, better textures, especially to avoid pattern repetition, which is so evident from  above.

- Sound: I think this is especially important for immersion.  Ambient sounds volume on planets is very dim (sea, wind, etc..).  Weapon sounds are very cartoonish in my opinion.  It would be nice to have also footsteps/running sounds, backpack pickup, weapon storing, etc...

- More weapons/ships (I’m sure this is coming).

- Weight/Gravity: objects (including animated characters) feel weightless.  It would be great to have a more realistic sense of acceleration in free-falling bodies.  Ships feel they’re made of aluminum foil, you can just push them around with your hand!

- Stamina/Hunger: a system like in Saints and Sinners or Into the Radius, where you get tired and are required to eat to recover stamina.

- Possibility to by property in different planets so you can stay there and rest.

- A more complex weather system on planets, with cloud variability from sunny to fully covered, rain, snow, etc.  A more realistic day/night cycle like in Into the Radius.

- Create a small Concert Hall where you can go and listen to music, relax, etc.  You can add an option of “naming a chair”, as a donation system to the dev.

This game can be epic! Keep up the good work.  I look forward to more.

As the developer has made clear, this game is far from finished so it seems odd to review it 5 stars now. The reason I did is because everything that is here so far is amazing and if development stays on this track, Revria could easily become my (and many others) favourite quest game. 

 All the mechanics are very well thought out and implemented. There are so many clever little touches.

 The ships control panel is simple but so evective and I find myself switching between control methods depending on my situation. Having to make these little adjustments really adds to the feeling of piloting a ship as well as allowing for very precise control. 

The holomap looks great and has an awesome sci fi feel and I love that you will need to scout for charging stations or shops using it. Planning ahead by charging your ship and buying weapons really gives a sense of surviving, but without ever being tedious or intrusive. 

The graphics are surprisingly detailed in places and the cities and ships looks great
The planets look amazing and drip atmosphere. Even with unfinished NPC's the words somehow feel real with their various habitats  and vistas. Planning a bounty mission by gearing up, traveling through space and there navagating a planets surface makes me feel like I'm in a combination of Blade Runner and the Mandalorian

All in all this is the game I've been looking for for a long while - or at the start of it at least. A great space survival game without the horrific No Mans Sky grind. The developer is clearly very talented and I really hope this game continues to grow.

reviews.review_title: Not finshed, but has a ton of potential!
reviews.review_description:
This game is a weird one to review, because it's clearly not finished. That's not a problem though, it's clearly marked as Early Access so that's to be expected. 

What is here at the moment is a sandbox in the vain of a GTA, only with a sci-fi twist. You're on a planet that you're free to explore, either on foot or by flying car.

At the moment there's only simplistic missions objectives to achieve, and a bunch of NPCs all over the place to "interact"... as in shoot at or ram into. Commit a crime though and the cops will hunt you down. All this happen through dynamic systems that are fun to play around with. What's here is a neat start however, it's a fun world to just mess around in. If there was more content as in usable items/weapons and missions to do - it could be incredible. I have high hopes for what this could become!

This game has great bones! The basics are there the game just needs more refining. If you have a chance to support this game go ahead and buy, it'll be worth it in the long run. The more support they get the better this game will be, it's slated to be an awesome game. The flight mechanics are so easy and fun, the views in space look amazing! The wrist ui is so simple and perfect for easy navigating. I'd love to see more detailed ships and buildings. Enjoy!

reviews.review_title: Nothing Wrong to buy that game  !
reviews.review_description:
I am playing now about 5 hours and most important i am looking forward to the next 5. 
the Game earned my 5 star rating not because it is perekt . 5 stars i give when the core game is fun for me and this is definitly the case here. You start and do some delivery jobs . Sounds boring but it isnt. The flying and the oriantation in the game is so much fun and it is very intuitiv. If you earned money or found some weapons you take on the first bounty missions and the fun factor here is getting higher and higher.  My plan for the next sessions is to earn enough money to affort a better ship to be able to accept the space fight missions. The Dev , i here it is only one person , did a very good job here.  What i would like to see is a sort of trading system between the planets and more houses to enter .

Ok iam playing now about 15 hours and still love it.  The only complaint i have is that there is nothing left to do for me. I got over a 150.000 cash and cant invest anywhere. Please add stuff to upgrade ship and weapons. Pls give me a reason to play on, because it is superb !!!

reviews.review_title: THIS IS GOING TO BE SPECIAL
reviews.review_description:
OK so the world isn't massively populated with stuff to do yet and the graphics are charmingly low textured however, this feel great to play. To know that you can walk in to a building and go inside or fly a vehicle at your will is crazy on a quest. It's going to become a very special game. Please support this! Quest needs experimental stuff like this.

The closest we've got to GTA so far

reviews.review_title: This is going to be "Star Citizen VR"
reviews.review_description:
This is going to be "Star Citizen VR" for Meta Quest 

Yes it's in early access, but what you get is a whole star system with Planets, different types of vehicles, npc's and bots roaming around, cities, settlements and so much more and it's all made by one Dev 

It's an "Open World/ Solar System full of exploring, Bounty hunting, package delivery and fun 

It's physics based, the core gaming system is already in place, so much more will be added in the coming months 

You can customize your character skin color, cloths etc, you start with your basic flying pod, you can adjust the flight controls to your liking, which makes it easier to fly 

You can do delivery jobs Planet to Planet, or across the other side of the planet

You can also do Bounty jobs, (you'll need a gun) you'll have to accept contacts and do your job to get paid 

The graphics are nice for the quest (place holders) graphics will be upgraded it's planned 

As I stated above, it's in "early access", this game has a "Road map" that's has what's coming and it's going to be epic 

Before you start the game you can select the Planet your from and you have your own apartment 

You can join the discord server to add to the suggestions, on what you would like to see implemented in the game

This will be the "Star Citizen VR" for Quest if the Dev add multiplayer, time will tell.


I'm giving it 5 stars because this game is addictive and what's coming next is going to change the vr gaming world for Quest players

This game is fun and I like the pay off the debt thing but I wish their was a way to play and shoot and bla bla bla with out having to pay money cause I’ve been playing it and I don’t know how to even get money yet other then selling guns or what criminals drop when they get killed but it’s is a very fun game and I’m still figuring it out but definitely buy this game so worth it and the guns should be less wobbly but that’s all the pros and cons in my opinion but yeah buy this game plz
This game has potential to be amazing. Right now its bare bones but hopefully the developer works hard on it and it becomes amazing. This is why we have app lab stuff. Support this developer!

reviews.review_title: A hidden gem of a game
reviews.review_description: This is a very interesting game. Sure the graphics may not look as pretty as other games on Quest but i think they are very charming. The game reminds me of Outer Wilds a little, but i think that's just how scaled everything is what reminds me of it. This is very promising game, i hope they add more jobs in it like mining where you mine asteroids for resources to sell for money for example. Or some stuff you could buy for your apartment. I like the sandbox feeling of the game and wish there was more stuff to do or mess around with. For example i found an arcade which made me think there's some games to play inside but it wasn't accessible. I do apprechiate the planets/moon actually orbiting each other. I was on the moon of the Earth-like planet and it was amazing to see things like the sun go over the hill and things like the planets slowly but surely going through the sky. I do wish tho that the towns and cities on the planets could look a little better. Adding things like roads, highways or pathways would sell the ideas of these being actual cities than just random assets placed around the planet.

reviews.review_title: For me one of the most promising VRgames
reviews.review_description:
You can feel the developer's love for his game in every single Pixel!

If you love Space Games like Elite, No man's or Star Citizen in Meta Quest style, if you love freedom and Sandbox games and want to support a very talented Developer MUST buy this game!

I don't know a single Quest game that offers you this freedom in VR Standalone. We're not talking about open Areas or open Cities here... You can explore entire Planets, even an entire Planetary System, completely freely, which for me is a real miracle do to the limited Quest hardware. 

The Controls are very well done in my opinion. Many of the sounds so far are more like placeholders, the graphics are functional but the immersion and the feeling of freedom is great. The Ships have a very special charm and new Vehicles will be added as development progresses, but I hope that the old ones continue to exist in thr Game. 

The Roadmap for Revria gives an idea of ​​what a great Game is being created here! The developer is constantly present in Discord and takes the wishes of his community very seriously. Everyone is eagerly awaiting the next big Update.

I wish the Dev added Landing Gear for the Ships, a Jetpack would also be incredible AND Quest 3 graphic optimization would be amazing in the Future. 
The developer already has everything else that I'm missing on his roadmap.

You really get a very special Quest title for little money!

reviews.review_title: Best flying game I have ever played!!
reviews.review_description: So fun to fly around listening to the amazing ambient music. So great after a stressful day!
This game is like daydreaming. A must have.

Spent a good amount of hours messing around in this game. It’s amazing that such a huge open world can run on quest. The game is in early access so keep that in mind before you buy. I’m excited to see how this game improves in the future
